The '''two-wave gravshock''', also known as the '''gravshock device''' or simply the '''planetbuster''', was an Imperial [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] that manipulated gravity on a planetary scale.

==Description==
Designed by the [[Imperial Department of Military Research/Legends|Imperial Department of Military Research]], the two-wave gravshock was a large, power-hungry apparatus housed within the shell of a [[Torpedo Sphere/Legends|Torpedo Sphere]]. The device used [[gravwave]]s to affect a planet's [[Gravity/Legends|gravitic]] field, thus causing effects that resembled [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquakes]] and other natural disasters. The device therefore could cause cities to be crushed in upon themselves or flooded by massive waves.

During development, it was discovered that the effect of low-level gravwave broadcasts could also be used to determine the interior composition of a [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]], offering non-military applications in exploration and mining.

==History==
Two problems were discovered during the testing phase of the project. The initial prototypes were carried within the hulls of modified [[Torpedo Sphere/Legends|Torpedo Spheres]], which proved capable of mounting attacks on city-sized targets; but only the drives of [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]] were thought to have sufficient power to mount attack on a fully planetary scale, and a Super Star Destroyer had no need of such a device to supplement its [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] and [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] batteries. Perhaps equally seriously, the violent disruption which the device caused to the target planet's gravitational field during an attack created severe navigational problems for ships in orbit, including the attacking vessel itself, and any escorts it might have.
